openmeter/billing/charges/flatfee/servic.purpose: Owns flat-fee credit allocation and correction mechanics: persisting credit realization records and their lineage segments. It must not make state-machine decisions — all charge lifecycle transitions happen in the parent charges.Service layer.

patterns.name: Config struct with Validate() before construction
patterns.description: All dependencies are declared in a Config struct; Config.Validate() collects all nil-check errors via errors.Join before New() returns a *Service. Never construct Service directly.
patterns.example: func New(config Config) (*Service, error) { if err := config.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, err } ... }

patterns.name: Input structs carry a Validate() method
patterns.description: Each method input (AllocateCreditsOnlyInput, CorrectAllCreditRealizationsInput) has a Validate() that checks zero-values and negative amounts before any adapter call. Call Validate() at the top of every service method.
patterns.example: if err := in.Validate(); err != nil { return AllocateCreditsOnlyResult{}, err }

patterns.name: Currency rounding before validation
patterns.description: RoundToPrecision is called on monetary amounts before Validate() to avoid false negatives from floating-point comparisons. Pattern: round first, then validate, then assert sum equality.
patterns.example: in.Amount = in.CurrencyCalculator.RoundToPrecision(in.Amount); if err := in.Validate(); err != nil { ... }

patterns.name: Lineage persistence is always paired with adapter writes
patterns.description: CreateCreditAllocations calls adapter.CreateCreditAllocations, then lineage.CreateInitialLineages, then lineage.PersistCorrectionLineageSegments — all three must succeed or the caller's transaction rolls back. Never skip lineage steps.

patterns.name: Handler delegates external credit allocation decisions
patterns.description: The Service does not compute how credits are split — it delegates to flatfee.Handler.OnCreditsOnlyUsageAccrued / OnCreditsOnlyUsageAccruedCorrection. Service only validates sum equality after the fact.

patterns.name: Sum equality assertion after allocation
patterns.description: After handler returns allocations, the sum is re-rounded and checked against the original amount. Mismatch returns models.NewGenericValidationError, not an internal error.
patterns.example: if !allocated.Equal(in.Amount) { return ..., models.NewGenericValidationError(fmt.Errorf("credit allocations do not match total ...")) }

key_file_guides.role: The entire package is this single file. Defines Service struct, Config, all input/result types, and three exported methods: CreateCreditAllocations, AllocateCreditsOnly, CorrectAllCredits.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding state-machine logic here violates the layer contract — state transitions belong in the parent charges service. Also watch for missing lineage.PersistCorrectionLineageSegments calls after adapter writes.

anti_patterns: Making state-machine decisions (charge status transitions) inside this package — it must remain a pure realization mechanics layer
Calling adapter.CreateCreditAllocations without following up with both lineage.CreateInitialLineages and lineage.PersistCorrectionLineageSegments
Skipping Validate() on input structs before adapter calls
Performing currency arithmetic without rounding via CurrencyCalculator.RoundToPrecision first
Returning a raw internal error instead of models.NewGenericValidationError when allocation sums don't match

decisions.decision: Service delegates allocation-splitting to flatfee.Handler rather than computing it internally
decisions.rationale: Different flat-fee billing strategies (immediate full allocation vs. pro-rated) need to vary the split logic without touching realization persistence code.

decisions.decision: Lineage persistence is mandatory and co-located in CreateCreditAllocations
decisions.rationale: Realization records without lineage segments break correction lookups in CorrectAllCredits; coupling the two writes in one method makes it impossible to forget lineage.

decisions.decision: Config+Validate pattern instead of variadic options
decisions.rationale: Consistent with the broader billing/charges constructor pattern; makes missing-dependency errors surface at startup rather than at first call.

code_examples.scenario: Allocate credits for a flat-fee charge amount, persist realizations and lineage
